questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}$ be three coplanar unit vectors such that $\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}=\overrightarrow{0}$. If three vectors $\vec{p}, \vec{q}, \vec{r}$ parallel to $\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}$ respectively and having integral but different magnitudes, then among the following options $|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{p}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{q}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}|$ can take a value equal to
questions.solution:
Let $\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}$ lie in the xy plane
Let $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}, \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=-\frac{1}{2} \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \hat{\mathrm{j}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}=-\frac{1}{2} \hat{i}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \hat{\mathrm{j}}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore|\vec{p}+\vec{q}+\vec{r}|=|\lambda \vec{a}+\mu \vec{b}+v \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|=\left|\lambda \hat{i}+\mu\left(-\frac{1}{2} \hat{i}+\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \hat{j}\right)+v\left[-\frac{1}{2} \hat{i}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \hat{j}\right]\right| \\
& =\sqrt{\left(\lambda-\frac{\mu}{2}-\frac{v}{2}\right)^2+\frac{3}{4}(\mu-v)^2}=\sqrt{\lambda^2+\mu^2+v^2-\lambda \mu-\lambda v-\mu v} \\
& =\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sqrt{(\lambda-\mu)^2+(\mu-v)^2+(v-\lambda)^2} \geq \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \sqrt{1+1+4}=\sqrt{3}
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{p}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{q}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}|$ can take a value equal to $\sqrt{3}$ and 2 .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider the points $A(\hat{o}), B(3 \hat{i}+f(t) \hat{j}+\hat{k})$ and $C\left(2 \hat{i}+f^{\prime}(t) \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}\right)$ and another point $D$ are coplanar for all $t \in R$
options: if $D$ is $(\hat{i})$ then $f(x)=c e^{2 x}, c \in R$
if $D$ is ( i$)$ then $f(x)=c \ln (2 x), c \in R$
if $D$ is $(\hat{i}+\hat{j})$ then $f(x)=2+c e^{2 x}, x \in R$
if $D$ is $(\hat{i}+\hat{j})$ then $f(x)=2+c \ln (2 x), c \in R$
questions.solution:
Four point A, B, C, D are coplanar if three vector $\overrightarrow{A B}, \overrightarrow{A C}, \overrightarrow{A D}$ are coplanar or STP of there vectors is 0
$\overrightarrow{A B} \cdot(\overrightarrow{A C} \times \overrightarrow{A D})=0$
$\Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 3 & f(t) & 1 \\ 2 & f^{\prime}(t) & 2\end{array}\right|=0 \quad \forall t \in R$
$\Rightarrow f(t)=2 f(t)$
$\Rightarrow f(t)=c^{2 t}$
Similarly for $\dot{B}(i+j)$ differential equation will be $f^{\prime}(t)-2 f(t)=-4$ On solving $f(x)=2+c e^{2 x}, c \in R$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider the points $A(\vec{o}), B(3 \hat{i}+f(t) \hat{j}+\hat{k})$ and $C\left(2 \hat{i}+f^{\prime}(t) \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}\right)$ and another point $D$ are coplanar for all $t \in R$
options: if $D$ is $(\hat{i})$ then $f(x)=c e^{2 x}, c \in R$
if $D$ is $(\hat{i})$ then $f(x)=c \ln (2 x), c \in R$
if $D$ is $(\hat{i}+\hat{j})$ then $f(x)=2+c e^{2 x}, x \in R$
if $D$ is $(\hat{i}+\hat{j})$ then $f(x)=2+c \ln (2 x), c \in R$
questions.solution:
Four point $A, B, C, D$ are coplanar if three vector $\overrightarrow{A B}, \overrightarrow{A C}, \overrightarrow{A D}$ are coplanar or STP of there vectors is 0
$\begin{aligned}
& \overrightarrow{\mathrm{AB}} \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{AC}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{AD}})=0 \\
& \Rightarrow\left|\begin{array}{ccc}
1 & 0 & 0 \\
3 & f(t) & 1 \\
2 & f^{\prime}(t) & 2
\end{array}\right|=0 \quad \forall t \in R \\
& \Rightarrow f^{\prime}(t)=2 f(t) \\
& \Rightarrow f(t)=c e^{2 t}
\end{aligned}$
Similarly for $\vec{B}(\hat{i}+\hat{j})$ differential equation will be $f^{\prime}(t)-2 f(t)=-4$
On solving $f(x)=2+c e^{2 x}, c \in R$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: In a four - dimensional space where unit vectors along axes are $\hat{i}, \hat{j}, \hat{\mathrm{k}}$ and $\hat{\ell}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_1, \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_2, \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_3, \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_4$ are four non-zero vectors such that no vector can be expressed as linear combination of others and $(\lambda-1)\left(\vec{a}_1-\vec{a}_2\right)+\mu\left(\vec{a}_2+\vec{a}_3\right)+\gamma\left(\vec{a}_3+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_4-2 \vec{a}_2\right)+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_3+\delta \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_4=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{o}}$ then
questions.solution:
$(\lambda-1)\left(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_1-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_2\right)+\mu\left(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_2+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_3\right)+\gamma\left(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_3+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_4-2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_2\right)+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_3+\delta \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}_4=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{o}}$
i.e. $(\lambda-1) \vec{a}_1+(1-\lambda+\mu-2 \gamma) \vec{a}_2+(\mu+\gamma+1) \vec{a}_3+(\gamma+\delta) \vec{a}_4=\vec{o}$
Since $\vec{a}_1, \vec{a}_2, \vec{a}_3, \vec{a}_4$ are linearly independent
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \lambda=1=0,1-\lambda+\mu-2 \gamma=0, \mu+\gamma+1=0, \gamma+\delta=0 \\ & \text { i.e. } \lambda=1, \mu=2 \gamma, \mu+\gamma+1=0, \gamma+\delta=0 \\ & \text { i.e. } \lambda=1, \mu=-\frac{2}{3}, \gamma=-\frac{1}{3}, \delta=\frac{1}{3}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: If $O A B C$ is a tetrahedron (where $O$ is origin and points $A, B, C$ lies on $x, y, z$ axes respectively). Let $A^{\prime}$ is point on $\mathrm{OA}(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}), \mathrm{B}^{\prime}$ is point on $\mathrm{OB}(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}) \& \mathrm{C}^{\prime}$ is point on $\mathrm{OC}(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})$ such that $A^{\prime} \mathrm{B}^{\prime} \mathrm{C}^{\prime}$ plane is parallel to ABC and volume of tetrahedron $\mathrm{OA}^{\prime} \mathrm{B}^{\prime} \mathrm{C}^{\prime}$ is half the volume of tetrahedron OABC , then -
options: equation of plane through three points $A^{\prime}, B^{\prime}, C^{\prime}$ is $\frac{x}{|\vec{a}|}+\frac{y}{|\vec{b}|}+\frac{z}{|\vec{c}|}=\frac{1}{2}$
equation of plane through three points $A^{\prime}, B^{\prime}, C^{\prime}$ is $\frac{x}{|\vec{a}|}+\frac{y}{|\vec{b}|}+\frac{z}{|\vec{c}|}=\frac{1}{2^{1 / 3}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}$ be three non zero vectors satisfying the condition $\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c} \& \vec{b} \times \vec{c}=\vec{a}$ then which of the following always hold(s) good?
questions.solution:
Clearly $\vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=0$ and $\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}=0$ Also $\vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=0 \Rightarrow A$
$\vec{a} \times \vec{b}=\vec{c}$
$\operatorname{dot}$ with $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}=0$
Similarly $\vec{b} \times \vec{c}=\vec{a}$
dot with $\vec{b} \Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=0$; dot with $\vec{c} \Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{c}=0$
$\Rightarrow \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}=\vec{b} \cdot \vec{c}=\vec{c} \cdot \vec{a}=0$
$\text { Again } \begin{aligned}
& |\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}| \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}| \\
& |\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}||\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}|
\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{|\vec{a}|}{|\vec{c}|}=\frac{|\vec{c}|}{|\vec{a}|} \Rightarrow|\vec{a}|=|\vec{c}| \&|\vec{b}|=1$
$\Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}||\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}||\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}|^2=|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}$ are non zero vectors such that they are not orthogonal pairwise and such that $\vec{V}_1=\vec{a} \times(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})$ and $\vec{V}_2=(\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \times \vec{c}$. If $\vec{V}_1=\vec{V}_2$ then which of the following hold(s) good?
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \overrightarrow{\mathrm{V}}_1=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{V}}_2 \\
& \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})=(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}) \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \\
& (\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}) \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}-(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{~b}}) \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}=(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}) \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}-(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}) \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \\
& \therefore \quad(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{~b}}) \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}=(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}) \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}
\end{aligned}$
$\Rightarrow$ Either $\vec{c}$ and $\vec{a}$ are collinear or $\vec{b}$ is perpendicular to both $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{c} \Rightarrow \vec{b}=\lambda(\vec{a} \times \vec{c})$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An equilateral triangle $\triangle O A B$ has side length $1, P$ is a point on the plane of the triangle. If $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{O P}}=(2-t) \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O A}}+t \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O B}}, t \in R$, then the possible value of $|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A P}}|$ can be:
questions.solution:
Let $A$ is $(1,0), B$ is $\left(\frac{1}{2}, \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$ and $P$ is $(x, y)$
$\begin{array}{ll}\Rightarrow & \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O P}}=(2-t) \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O A}}+t \overrightarrow{\mathbf{O B}}, t \in R \\ \Rightarrow & x \hat{\mathbf{i}}+y \hat{\mathbf{j}}=(2-t) \hat{\mathbf{i}}+t\left(\frac{\hat{\mathbf{i}}}{2}+\frac{\sqrt{3} \hat{\mathbf{j}}}{2}\right)\end{array}$
$\therefore \quad x=2-\frac{t}{2} ; y=\frac{\sqrt{3} t}{2}$
$|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A P}}|=|(x-1) \hat{\mathbf{i}}+y \hat{\mathbf{j}}|=\sqrt{(x-1)^2+y^2}=\sqrt{t^2-t+1} \geq \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Let $\vec{x}=\hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-2 \hat{k}, \vec{y}=2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}+\hat{k}$ and a vector $\vec{z}$ satisfying $\vec{x} \times \vec{z}=\vec{x} \times \vec{y}$ and $\vec{z} \cdot \vec{x}=0$. Then $[|\vec{z}|]$ is greater than, (where [.] denotes greatest integer function)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}}=2 \hat{\mathbf{i}}+\hat{\mathbf{j}}+5 \hat{\mathbf{k}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}}=x \hat{\mathbf{i}}+y \hat{\mathbf{j}}+z \hat{\mathbf{k}}$
If $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}}=11 ; \overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}}=-13 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-9 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+7 \hat{\mathbf{k}}$, then:
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution:
$\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}}=-13 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-9 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+7 \hat{\mathbf{k}}$
Take cross with $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}}$
$\therefore \quad \overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}} \times(\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}})=\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}} \times(-13 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-9 \hat{\mathbf{j}}+7 \hat{\mathbf{k}})$
$(\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}}) \overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}}-|\overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}}|^2 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}}=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\hat{\mathbf{i}} & \hat{\mathbf{j}} & \hat{\mathbf{k}} \\ 2 & 1 & 5 \\ -13 & -9 & 7\end{array}\right|=52 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-79 \hat{\mathbf{j}}-5 \hat{\mathbf{k}}$
$\begin{aligned} & 11 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{A}}-30 \overrightarrow{\mathbf{B}}=52 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-79 \hat{\mathbf{j}}-5 \hat{\mathbf{k}} \\ & (22-30 x) \hat{\mathbf{i}}+(11-30 y) \hat{\mathbf{j}}+(55-30 z) \hat{\mathbf{k}}=52 \hat{\mathbf{i}}-79 \hat{\mathbf{j}}-5 \hat{\mathbf{k}}\end{aligned}$
Now, comparing coefficients of $\hat{\mathbf{i}}, \hat{\mathbf{j}}$ and $\hat{\mathbf{k}}$, we get
$\begin{array}{lll}22-30 x=52 & \Rightarrow & x=-1 \\ 11-30 y=-79 & \Rightarrow & y=3 \\ 55-30 z=-5 & \Rightarrow & z=2\end{array}$
Now, verify.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\vec{a}, \vec{b}$ and $\vec{c}$ be three unit vectors such that $|\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}|=\sqrt{3}$ and $(\vec{a} \times \vec{b}) \cdot(\vec{b} \times \vec{c})+(\vec{b} \times \vec{c}) \cdot(\vec{c} \times \vec{a})+(\vec{c} \times \vec{a}) \cdot(\vec{a} \times \vec{b})=\lambda$. Which of the following are CORRECT ?
options: If $\lambda$ is maximum then the volume of parallelepiped determined by $\vec{a}, \vec{b}$ and $\vec{c}$ is $\sqrt{3}$
If $\lambda$ is maximum then the value of $|(2 \vec{a}+3 \vec{b}+4 \vec{c}) \cdot(\vec{a} \times \vec{b}+5 \vec{b} \times \vec{c}+6 \vec{c} \times \vec{a})|$ is 32 .
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & (\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}) \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})=(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}})-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \\ & (\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}) \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})=(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \\ & (\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}) \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}})=(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}})(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}\end{aligned}$
Given that
$\begin{aligned} & |\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}|=\sqrt{3} \Rightarrow(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}) \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})=3 \Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}+\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}=0 \\ & \lambda=(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}) \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})+(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})+(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}})-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \\ & \Rightarrow \lambda=(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}) \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})+(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})+(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}) \\ & \Rightarrow \lambda \leq 0[\text { since }, x+y+z=0, x y+y z+\mathrm{xx} \leq 0] \\ & \lambda_{\max }=0 \text { only when } \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \perp \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}, \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \perp \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \text { and } \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \perp \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \\ & (2 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}+3 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{~b}}+4 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}) \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}}+5 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{~b}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}}+6 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}}) \\ & =10 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}})+18 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{~b}} \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}})+4 \overrightarrow{\mathrm{c}} \cdot(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{a}} \times \overrightarrow{\mathrm{b}})=32\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f: R \rightarrow(0,1)$ be a continuous function, then which of the following pair of vectors are linearly dependent for some $x \in(0,1)$ ?
questions.solution:
(1) For linearly dependent $\frac{f(x)}{x^2}=\frac{2}{3} \Rightarrow 3 f(x)-2 x^2=0$
Let $g(x)=3 f(x)-2 x^2$
$\therefore \quad g(0)=3 f(0)>0$ and $g(1)=3 f(1)-2$, may be positive or negative
(2) $\frac{f(x)}{x^2}=\frac{3}{2} \Rightarrow 2 f(x)-3 x^2=0$
Let $g(x)=2 f(x)-3 x^2$
$g(0)=2 f(0)>0$ and $g(1)=2 f(1)-3 < 0$
$\therefore \quad \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}$ are linearly dependent.
(3) $\frac{\int_0^{1-x} f(t) d t}{x}=\frac{3}{2} \Rightarrow 2 \int_0^{1-x} f(t) d t-3 x=0$
Let $g(x)=2 \int_0^{1-x} f(t) d t-3 x$
$\therefore \quad g(0)=2 \int_0^1 f(t) d t>0$ and $g(1)=-3 < 0$
$\therefore \quad \overrightarrow{\mathbf{a}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\mathbf{b}}$ are linearly dependent.
Similarly for (4) also vectors are linearly dependent.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct :
options: If $\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}$ are non-coplanar and $\vec{d}$ is any vector, then $[\vec{d} \vec{b} \vec{c}] \vec{a}+[\vec{d} \vec{c} \vec{a}] \vec{b}+[\vec{d} \vec{a} \vec{b}] \vec{c}-[\vec{a} \vec{b} \vec{c}] \vec{d}=\overrightarrow{0}$
If $I$ is incentre of $\triangle A B C$ then $|\overrightarrow{B C}| \overrightarrow{I A}+|\overrightarrow{C A}| \overrightarrow{I B}+|\overrightarrow{A B}| \overrightarrow{I C}=\overrightarrow{0}$
Any vector in three dimension can be written as linear combination of three non-coplanar vectors.
In a triangle, if position vector of vertices are $\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}$, then position vector of incentre is $\frac{\vec{a}+\vec{b}+\vec{c}}{3}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The position vectors of the angular points of a tetrahedron are
$A(3 \hat{i}-2 \hat{j}+\hat{k}), B(3 \hat{i}+\hat{j}+5 \hat{k})$
$C(4 \hat{i}+3 \hat{k})$ and $D(\hat{i})$. Then the acute angle between the lateral face ADC and the base face $A B C$ is:
questions.solution:
$\overrightarrow{A D}=-2 \hat{i}+2 \hat{j}-\hat{k}, \overrightarrow{A C}=\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}+2 \hat{k}$
$\therefore$ vector perpendicular to the face $A D C$ is $=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}\hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ -2 & 2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & 2\end{array}\right|=6 \hat{i}+3 \hat{j}-6 \hat{k} \Rightarrow \overrightarrow{A B}=3 \hat{j}+4 \hat{k}$
$\therefore A$ vector perpendicular to the face $A B C$ is $=\left|\begin{array}{lll}\hat{i} & \hat{j} & \hat{k} \\ 0 & 3 & 4 \\ 1 & 2 & 2\end{array}\right|=-2 \hat{i}+4 \hat{j}-3 \hat{k}$
$\therefore \quad$ acute angle between the two faces is given by $\quad \cos \theta=\left|\frac{-12+12+18}{\sqrt{36+9+36} \sqrt{4+16+9}}\right|=\frac{2}{\sqrt{29}}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \therefore \quad \tan \theta=\frac{5}{2} \\
& \therefore \quad \theta=\tan ^{-1} \frac{5}{2}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
